source.title: Hippocampal place cells construct reward related sequences through unexplored space

confidence_rationale: Direct place-cell recording in rats showing that offline replay seeds can correspond to never-visited portions of the environment when those portions are motivationally relevant. Strongly supports the broad-coverage reading of replay seeding, but the gating signal here is visible reward (salience), not accumulated epistemic staleness per se -- MECH-285's dissociation claim is adjacent but not directly isolated. Mapping to MECH-285 leans on the broader structural finding (replay reaches beyond visited anchors) rather than the specific priority signal.
failure_signatures: If the sleep-replay seed distribution were strictly confined to active (recently-visited) anchors, preplay of an unvisited-but-rewarded arm should not occur at above-chance rates; Olafsdottir et al. show that it does.
The observed gating signal is visible reward, which composes with but is not identical to the epistemic-staleness signal MECH-285 posits; the study does not dissociate the two.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Viewing the delivery of food to an unvisited portion of an environment leads to off-line pre-activation of place-cell sequences corresponding to that space; no such preplay arises for an unrewarded but otherwise similar portion.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-285's start-state distribution for offline replay need not be confined to active/visited anchors. A motivational gating signal (here, visible reward) expands the seed pool to include places the agent has a reason to model but has not yet physically sampled. For MECH-285 this suggests that the broad-coverage reading is biologically available: the sleep consumer can be seeded from regions that are not currently 'active' in the narrow visited-trajectory sense.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The gating variable in Olafsdottir et al. is visible reward, which maps onto dopaminergic salience-tagging (MECH-074b / McNamara 2014 substrate) more cleanly than onto epistemic V_s staleness. MECH-285 predicts a distinct, dissociable staleness arm; this study establishes that *some* broad-coverage mechanism exists but does not pin down whether the epistemic arm is separable from salience.
mapping.source_context: Rat hippocampus (CA1), behaving/resting; T-maze variant in which one arm is visible but barriered from physical access and contains (or does not contain) visible food.

confidence_components.notes: Strong single-unit electrophysiology in a behaviourally clean paradigm. Mapping fidelity drops because the salience/epistemic-staleness dissociation that MECH-285 lives or dies by is not directly tested -- the study speaks to broad vs narrow seed coverage, not to the priority shape or dissociability from dopamine.
